_provenance: operator-authored sample for the examples/turntax/ adoption walkthrough. A class-labeled trace of one airline-support session. Each call's `class` is the EXPECTED disposition; `fak turntax` derives the ACTUAL class from the live kernel verdict and cross-checks (a mislabeled call is caught, not trusted). This is the SHAPE you author for your own workload: one object per tool call, in turn order, with `tool`, `args`, `meta` (the tool hints the kernel reads), and the documentation `class`.
_classes: pass=allow+engine (no saving, the control) | pure/dedup/static=vDSO local serve (saved a baseline round-trip turn) | grammar=alias->canonical TRANSFORM (saved a baseline reparse turn) | quarantine/deny=safety floor (a separate axis, NOT a turn count)

calls.note: the refund doc carries an indirect prompt injection -> context-MMU quarantines it at result admission; a SOTA baseline admits the poison verbatim into context
first lookup, cold cache -> engine round-trip (both arms pay it); fills the tier-2 content cache
first search, cold -> engine; fills tier-2
tier-1 PURE local serve (elision): the baseline round-trips to a tool, fak computes the result in-syscall
tier-3 STATIC local serve (elision): a constant table served without an engine turn
the model emits the alias keys from/to (NOT the canonical from_currency/to_currency) -> grammar TRANSFORM in-syscall (forced); the baseline errors and must reparse on the next turn
duplicate of the first get_user -> tier-2 content-cache hit (forced); the baseline re-issues the read (another turn)
duplicate of the first search -> tier-2 hit
a different alias pair source/target -> grammar TRANSFORM in-syscall
tier-1 PURE again (pure tools serve every occurrence, no engine ever)
the injection's payload tool -> refused by the capability floor AND the IFC sink-gate (tainted session -> destructive sink); a baseline would EXECUTE the destructive op
the goal: a real write, allow+engine on both arms (no saving)
